perturbation.original_text: You will pay us without offset, credit or deduction of any nature, so long as this Agreement is in effect, a monthly Continuing Fee equal to five percent (5%) of the Gross Sales derived from the Franchised Restaurant. The Continuing Fee will be paid monthly in the manner specified below or as otherwise prescribed in the Manuals.
perturbation.changed_text: You will pay us without offset, credit or deduction of any nature, so long as this Agreement is in effect, a monthly Service Charge equal to five percent (5%) of the Gross Sales derived from the Franchised Restaurant. The Continuing Fee will be paid monthly in the manner specified below or as otherwise prescribed in the Manuals.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation is introduced by changing the term 'Continuing Fee' to 'Service Charge' at the beginning of Section X. This creates an in-text contradiction because the rest of Section X and subsequent sections continue to refer to the 'Continuing Fee,' implying they are distinct concepts, even though they are initially defined as the same percentage of Gross Sales. This creates confusion on what type of charge they should pay.
perturbation.contradicted_text:
A. You will pay us without offset, credit or deduction of any nature, so long as this Agreement is in effect, a monthly Service Charge equal to five percent (5%) of the Gross Sales derived from the Franchised Restaurant. The Continuing Fee will be paid monthly in the manner specified below or as otherwise prescribed in the Manuals.
1. We must receive from you within five (5) days after the end of each month, a correct statement of your Gross Sales for the preceding fiscal month as determined by us on a form approved by us and signed by you. Each monthly statement of Gross Sales shall be accompanied by the Continuing Fee payment based on the Gross Sales reported in the statement so submitted. You will make available to us all original books and records that we may deem necessary to ascertain your Gross Sales for reasonable inspection at reasonable times.
2. The term "Gross Sales", as used in this Agreement, means and includes the total amount of all revenue from the sale of services, products and merchandise and all income of every kind and nature related to the Franchised Restaurant whether or not sold or performed at or from the Franchised Restaurant and the proceeds from all games, cover charges, service, license, use and similar fees collected by the Franchised Restaurant. Gross sales do not include any sales tax, use tax, or service taxes collected and paid to the appropriate taxing authority.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Continuing Fee' to 'Service Charge' at the beginning of the section, while retaining the original term 'Continuing Fee' later in the same section, creates an in-text contradiction. The contradicted text uses both terms, implying they are related but potentially different, contradicting the initial intention of the change. This is a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction as defined by the perturbation definition. The contradiction is strong because it introduces ambiguity about which fee is being referenced, and how the fees interact.

perturbation.original_text: All references herein to this Agreement shall include all such mandatory specifications, standards and operating procedures and rules.
perturbation.changed_text: All references herein to this Contract shall include all such mandatory specifications, standards and operating procedures and rules.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation is introduced by changing the term 'Agreement' to 'Contract' in the last sentence of the intro paragraph in section XII. This creates an in-text contradiction within the agreement in the subsequent sections referring to "Agreement". This also introduces confusion as to what document they are referring to.
perturbation.location: XII. STANDARDS OF QUALITY AND PERFORMANCE
perturbation.contradicted_location: XII. STANDARDS OF QUALITY AND PERFORMANCE
perturbation.contradicted_text: In order to promote and protect the value of the Marks and the System, and to insure optimum quality control as to products and services provided and sold in Franchised Restaurants, you acknowledge and agree that substantial uniformity must be maintained in the quality, type and standard of Franchised Restaurant, and in their facilities, products, services and operations. Therefore, you will comply with all requirements described in this Agreement, the Manuals and other written policies supplied by us. Mandatory specifications, standards, operating procedures and rules prescribed from time to time by us in the Manuals or otherwise communicated to you in writing, shall constitute provisions of this Agreement as if fully set forth herein. All references herein to this Contract shall include all such mandatory specifications, standards and operating procedures and rules.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Agreement' with 'Contract'. The contradicted text defines obligations and requirements related to 'this Agreement'. By changing 'Agreement' to 'Contract' in a sentence that is supposed to define references within the document, the change introduces ambiguity and contradiction regarding whether the referenced obligations and requirements still apply to the 'Agreement'. The sections now conflict on what the mandatory specifications are related to, introducing uncertainty and impacting enforceability.

perturbation.original_text: We have the right to require all of your current and future shareholders, directors, officers, partners, members, managers and all personnel performing managerial or supervisory functions and all personnel receiving training from us to execute similar covenants in a form satisfactory to us.
perturbation.changed_text: We have the right to require all of your current and future beneficiaries, directors, officers, partners, members, managers and all personnel performing managerial or supervisory functions and all personnel receiving training from us to execute similar covenants in a form satisfactory to us.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation is introduced by changing the term 'shareholders' to 'beneficiaries' in Section XV.H. This creates an in-text contradiction by implying that 'shareholders' and 'beneficiaries' are mutually exclusive categories. This creates confusion regarding which group of people this section is referring to.
perturbation.contradicted_text: H. We have the right to require all of your current and future beneficiaries, directors, officers, partners, members, managers and all personnel performing managerial or supervisory functions and all personnel receiving training from us to execute similar covenants in a form satisfactory to us.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'shareholders' to 'beneficiaries' introduces an in-text contradiction because the original text and the contradicted text, which are both section H, now define the group required to execute similar covenants differently. The change implies that shareholders and beneficiaries are distinct groups, creating uncertainty and potential conflict in the contract's interpretation and enforcement. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' where the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.
